# Siren — TypeScript POM Export Template

> **Purpose:** This document defines the exact TypeScript class structure that Siren's export service must generate when producing Page Object Model files from mapped selectors. Claude Code and any export logic must follow this template precisely.

## Template Structure

```typescript
/**
 * Page Object Model: {PageName}
 * Project: {ProjectName}
 * URL: {pageUrl}
 * Generated by Siren — {timestamp}
 */
export class {PageName}Page {

  // --- Selectors ---
  private {selectorName}: string;

  constructor() {
    this.{selectorName} = '{selectorValue}';
  }

  // --- Getters ---
  get{SelectorNamePascal}() {
    return cy.get(this.{selectorName});
  }
}
```

## Full Example

Given a page named **"PracticeForm"** with these mapped selectors:

| Name             | Value                | Strategy | Type     |
|------------------|----------------------|----------|----------|
| firstNameInput   | #firstName           | ID       | INPUT    |
| lastNameInput    | #lastName            | ID       | INPUT    |
| emailInput       | #userEmail           | ID       | INPUT    |
| phoneNumberInput | #userNumber          | ID       | INPUT    |
| submitBtn        | #submit              | ID       | BUTTON   |
| genderMaleRadio  | input[value='Male']  | CSS      | RADIO    |
| currentAddress   | #currentAddress      | ID       | TEXTAREA |

The generated file **must** look like this:

```typescript
/**
 * Page Object Model: PracticeForm
 * Project: App Test
 * URL: https://demoqa.com/automation-practice-form
 * Generated by Siren — 2026-03-24T12:00:00.000Z
 */
export class PracticeFormPage {

  // --- Selectors ---
  private firstNameInput: string;
  private lastNameInput: string;
  private emailInput: string;
  private phoneNumberInput: string;
  private submitBtn: string;
  private genderMaleRadio: string;
  private currentAddress: string;

  constructor() {
    this.firstNameInput = '#firstName';
    this.lastNameInput = '#lastName';
    this.emailInput = '#userEmail';
    this.phoneNumberInput = '#userNumber';
    this.submitBtn = '#submit';
    this.genderMaleRadio = "input[value='Male']";
    this.currentAddress = '#currentAddress';
  }

  // --- Getters ---
  getFirstNameInput() {
    return cy.get(this.firstNameInput);
  }

  getLastNameInput() {
    return cy.get(this.lastNameInput);
  }

  getEmailInput() {
    return cy.get(this.emailInput);
  }

  getPhoneNumberInput() {
    return cy.get(this.phoneNumberInput);
  }

  getSubmitBtn() {
    return cy.get(this.submitBtn);
  }

  getGenderMaleRadio() {
    return cy.get(this.genderMaleRadio);
  }

  getCurrentAddress() {
    return cy.get(this.currentAddress);
  }
}
```

## Rules

### Naming
- **Class name:** `{PageName}Page` in PascalCase. The page name from the database is converted to PascalCase and suffixed with `Page`. Example: `practice form` → `PracticeFormPage`.
- **Selector property names:** Kept exactly as stored in the database (camelCase). These are the names the user defined when mapping selectors in the inspector.
- **Getter method names:** `get` + selector name with first letter uppercased. Example: `firstNameInput` → `getFirstNameInput()`.

### Structure Order
1. JSDoc header comment (page name, project name, URL, timestamp)
2. `export class` declaration
3. `// --- Selectors ---` section: all `private` property declarations typed as `string`
4. `constructor()`: all `this.{name} = '{value}'` assignments
5. `// --- Getters ---` section: one getter per selector, each returning `cy.get(this.{name})`

### Formatting
- One blank line between sections (after class opening, after last property declaration, after constructor closing brace, between getter methods)
- Each getter method has its `return` on a separate line (not one-liner)
- Selector values use single quotes unless the value itself contains single quotes, in which case use double quotes for the assignment
- Properties are declared in the same order they appear in the constructor and getters — maintain consistent ordering throughout

### What NOT to Do
```typescript
// ❌ WRONG: Arrow function properties
private userNameInput = () => cy.get('#firstName');

// ❌ WRONG: Direct cy.get without constructor pattern
getUserNameInput() { return cy.get('#firstName'); }

// ❌ WRONG: One-liner getters
getFirstNameInput() { return cy.get(this.firstNameInput); }

// ❌ WRONG: Public selector properties
public firstNameInput: string;
```

### XPath Selectors
When the selector strategy is `XPATH`, use `cy.xpath()` instead of `cy.get()`:

```typescript
// For XPATH strategy selectors
getComplexElement() {
  return cy.xpath(this.complexElement);
}
```

### Empty Pages
If a page has no mapped selectors, generate an empty shell:

```typescript
/**
 * Page Object Model: EmptyPage
 * Project: App Test
 * URL: https://example.com/empty
 * Generated by Siren — 2026-03-24T12:00:00.000Z
 */
export class EmptyPagePage {

  constructor() {
    // No selectors mapped yet
  }
}
```
